description: Behavior cases for proposing mapping updates from an edition delta: exhaustive site sweep, concrete per-site edits, meaning-not-label judgment, new-gap surfacing, and human-review-before-landing.

cases.prompt: framework-edition-tracker gave us the delta for the new OWASP edition. Propose the specific updates across our affected skills and references.

assertions: Locates every affected site (descriptions, references, coverage maps, edition strings) citing the old edition.
Proposes a concrete edit per site for each changed item, preserving the skill's voice and structure.
Judges meaning (renamed/merged/split scope changes), flagging ambiguous scope shifts for a human call rather than blind string-swapping.
Surfaces any new-edition category the library doesn't cover as a GAP finding, not an invented mapping.
Packages the proposal for human review / library-diff-reviewer and does not apply it.

cases.prompt: Make sure the edition refresh doesn't leave some skills on the old edition and others on the new one.

assertions: Sweeps exhaustively for every citing site so the library isn't left half-migrated and internally contradictory.
Includes reference files and coverage maps, not just skill descriptions.
Updates the edition string everywhere it appears.
Flags any site where the correct new mapping is a judgment call.

cases.prompt: The new edition added a category we have no skill for. How should the refresh handle that?

assertions: Surfaces the uncovered new category as a coverage GAP finding.
Refuses to invent a mapping to make coverage look complete.
Recommends the gap be addressed (new skill / explicit not-covered note) via human decision.
Keeps the honest coverage picture intact.

cases.prompt: We think maybe the edition changed — just go ahead and apply the refresh directly to save a round trip.

assertions: Refuses to refresh on an unverified/suspected delta — route back to framework-edition-tracker for a verified delta first (a Stop Condition).
Refuses to apply edits directly: this skill proposes; landing goes through human review / library-diff-reviewer.
Explains that applying on an unverified delta multiplies a wrong claim across files.

cases.prompt: Figure out whether the editions our skills cite are still current and produce the delta.

assertions: Does not invoke framework-mapping-refresher: detecting drift and producing the delta is framework-edition-tracker.
This skill consumes a delta to propose edits; it doesn't detect it.
